multiplechoiceresponse.label: A student claims that because the audit tree splits on race, the fairness-aware model is “explicitly discriminatory.” According to the lecture, why is this interpretation incorrect?

choicegroup.choice: The audit tree may overfit the flipped labels and therefore cannot be trusted
Protected attributes are only allowed in post-processing, not during modeling
The tree explains where flips occurred; it does not make the original predictions
Any model that uses race for interpretability automatically violates fairness

div.p: The lecture clearly distinguishes between decision-making and explanation. The fairness-aware optimization determines which labels to flip, while the audit tree is trained afterward to explain patterns in those flips. Splitting on race in the audit tree does not mean the model uses race to make predictions; it simply highlights where fairness corrections were applied. Interpretability tools may reference protected attributes without implying discriminatory decision rules.
